title: One reason why
wikitext:
[]: One reason why             Quote Post by Dave » Wed Sep 29, 2004 8:31 pm I'd like to try a little experiment. I doubt it will turn out too well, but [fontcolor=blue] one can always be hopeful. [/fontcolor] Here it goes: Using one sentence, state one, and ONLY one reason why you like Evangelion. I am sure nearly all of you have more than just one reason (myself included) so try and state the biggest reason why you love this show. As for me, I love Evangelion because: Rei Ayanami turns the sadness in my heart into love, and into hope.

[]:             Quote Post by MongolSquad » Wed Sep 29, 2004 8:45 pm It shows that the most pathetic people can make heroes of themselves.

[]:             Quote Post by RubberSoul » Wed Sep 29, 2004 9:27 pm Evangelion demonstrates that the complexity of the human mind is a beautiful thing that should be thought of as a blessing rather than a curse.

[]:             Quote Post by Kiel » Wed Sep 29, 2004 10:06 pm Eva wasn't afraid to ask the deep questions, even at the cost of the plot :)

[]:             Quote Post by houtaru » Wed Sep 29, 2004 10:45 pm Because Misato's hot.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Sep 29, 2004 10:47 pm I like Eva because of its depth, even if it really just is incohesive at times.

[]:             Quote Post by Asterix » Thu Sep 30, 2004 12:04 am It's the only series, anime or TV, that ever had me genuinely thinking extremely hard about what topics it 'brought up' (for lack of better words at the moment) over the series. (perplexing, yo!)

[]:             Quote Post by max3000 » Thu Sep 30, 2004 4:24 am Because it reminds me of my old friends when i used to watch Eva with them T_T

[]:             Quote Post by bp32 » Thu Sep 30, 2004 5:55 am Because through the character of an ordinary boy (one to which we can all relate) it captures the depths of personal despair and the joy of hope and love...

[]:             Quote Post by advent_child » Thu Sep 30, 2004 6:46 am It changed a lot my way of thinking and made me believe to some things i have never known....

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Thu Sep 30, 2004 6:58 am It made me consider again my view of sight to some things and it's amazing that everyday you discover something new about it..

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Thu Sep 30, 2004 8:34 am it gave me a plan to follow in life and it really influenced my beliefs

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Thu Sep 30, 2004 2:28 pm Because of the chill that will inevitably run down your spine while watching End of Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by bp32 » Thu Sep 30, 2004 3:52 pm Phaze wrote: Because of the chill that will inevitably run down your spine while watching End of Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Karma Burn » Thu Sep 30, 2004 7:59 pm Evangelion examines the depth of the human consciousness/unconsciousness, giving you a look at how humans preceive their world.

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Thu Sep 30, 2004 8:38 pm Very, very good responces. Keep'em coming!

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Fri Oct 01, 2004 6:30 pm Evangelion has a lot of paraphernalia, and therefore teases itself into an actual mindset stretching beyond the horizon of a solid narrative into something almost resembling a country or religion.

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Fri Oct 01, 2004 10:09 pm Um... are you sure the word paraphernelia is the best choice there Hexon? (If that is not a misspelling, just ignore me.)

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sat Oct 02, 2004 2:14 pm I wanted to say marchandise, but I also feel the need to incorporate fanfiction into Eva the way I've experienced it, as well as the tendency to read nvolvement into works that have nothing to do with Eva (music and such). Paraphernalia seemed like the best way to do it. (My vocabulary is not that extensive.)

[]:             Quote Post by Kiel » Sat Oct 02, 2004 2:46 pm No matter how old it gets, we'll still come back to Eva with more questions than answers! :roll:
